fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Georgia Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Georgia - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity georgia, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to georgia, respiratory therapist compact georgia, georgia respiratory therapist endorsement application

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Georgia.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Register a new account on the Georgia Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal if you are a new user.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the Georgia Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal and complete the Respiratory Care Practitioner (RCP) application, selecting 'License by Endorsement' if applicable.

steps.title: Submit Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your approved Respiratory Care program to be sent directly from the educational institution to the Georgia Board of Respiratory Care.

steps.title: Verify NBRC Credentials
steps.description: Request official verification of your NBRC credentials (CRT and RRT) to be sent directly from the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) to the Georgia Board of Respiratory Care.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Register for and complete the required Georgia criminal background check via fingerprinting (LiveScan) as instructed by the Board.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable application fee of $75 through the Georgia Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Georgia respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Georgia have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Georgia. To work in Georgia, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Georgia?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Georgia while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Georgia
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Georgia, you must submit your application and supporting documents through the Georgia Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal.

official_links.portal_name: Georgia Professional Licensing Boards Online Services
official_links.registration_steps: Visit the Georgia Professional Licensing Boards Online Services portal. New users should click 'Register A New Account' to create credentials and begin the application process.
